description: Technical Mechanical Ethics registry for EthicBit / CEMU.

rules.description: All technical builds affecting critical outputs must be reproducible from preserved source, configuration, and dependency state.
rules.justification: Sin reproducibilidad técnica no existe verificación independiente fiable ni cierre de auditoría defendible.

rules.description: All critical technical transformations must be fully traceable across inputs, intermediate states, and outputs.
rules.justification: Sin trazabilidad técnica completa no es posible reconstruir causalidad, aislar fallos ni defender resultados ante auditoría.

rules.description: All technical verification procedures must preserve explainable failure reasons and actionable remediation paths.
rules.justification: Las fallas técnicas opacas impiden remediación disciplinada y degradan la capacidad de auditoría posterior.

rules.description: All detectable non-visible technical entities influencing critical outputs must preserve traceable technical evidence.
rules.reference: Constitutional amendment on expanded technical scope
rules.justification: Without detectable traceability, technically influential non-visible entities cannot be audited or governed.

rules.description: All agentic systems or multi-agent environments with material influence over critical outputs must remain auditable by interaction, orchestration, or reproducibility evidence.
rules.reference: Constitutional amendment on expanded technical scope
rules.justification: Agentic influence without auditable technical evidence undermines reproducibility, accountability, and technical trust.

rules.description: Any detectable non-visible technical entity affecting a critical path must trigger fail-closed behavior when detectability evidence is insufficient.
rules.reference: Constitutional amendment on expanded technical scope
rules.justification: Critical technical influence without sufficient detectability evidence is institutionally unsafe.

rules.description: Any detectable technical entity with legal, financial, security, or regulatory impact must trigger mandatory cross-sector review.
rules.reference: Constitutional amendment on expanded technical scope
rules.justification: Cross-domain impact cannot remain confined to a purely technical interpretation.
